name: ServiceNow, Inc.

profile.description: ServiceNow, Inc. is the leading enterprise workflow automation platform, providing cloud-based solutions for IT service management, HR service delivery, customer service, security operations, and AI-powered agentic workflows. The Now Platform serves as the system of action for enterprise digital transformation across Fortune 500 companies globally. Founded in 2004, led by Chairman and CEO Bill McDermott since 2019, headquartered in Santa Clara, California with a market cap of approximately $105 billion.

innovation.label: STRONG
innovation.analysis: Track Record (2/3): Under Bill McDermott (since 2019), ServiceNow has more than tripled full-year revenues from ~$3.5 billion to $13.3 billion and expanded from IT service management into HR delivery, customer service, security operations, and most recently AI-powered agentic workflows — each expansion opening significant new revenue streams [1][2]. The company crossed $10 billion in subscription revenue in 2024 with a 26% subscription revenue CAGR from 2020-2024, demonstrating sustained execution. AI Deployment (2/2): Now Assist generative AI is shipping and generating measurable revenue — customers spending over $1 million in annual contract value on Now Assist grew over 130% YoY in Q1 2026 [1]. ServiceNow launched AI Governance features to ensure enterprise AI agents operate within ethical and legal boundaries, including EU AI Act compliance [2]. Market Share (2/2): ServiceNow is the dominant enterprise workflow platform, recognized as a Leader in multiple Gartner Magic Quadrant categories including ITSM, strategic portfolio management, and enterprise service management. The company defines the category and has unmatched penetration across Global 2000 enterprises [2]. Product Pipeline (2/2): Full-year 2026 subscription revenue guidance raised to $15.74-15.78 billion (22-22.5% YoY growth), with remaining performance obligations of $27.7 billion (up 25% YoY) providing extraordinary revenue visibility [1]. Agentic AI capabilities, AI Governance, and RaptorDB database expansion have specific launch timelines. R&D Intensity (1/1): As a pure-play enterprise software company, ServiceNow's R&D spending significantly exceeds industry averages, with gross margins of 76.6% funding substantial engineering investment.

inspiration.label: DEVELOPING
inspiration.analysis: Mission Floor (3/4): ServiceNow's purpose — 'make the world work better for everyone' — is specific, thoughtful, and clearly shapes product strategy. It connects enterprise workflow automation to a broader vision of making everyone's work life more productive and fulfilling [2]. The purpose differentiates ServiceNow from generic enterprise software companies and is consistently referenced by McDermott in strategic decisions. However, at 22 years old, the company has not yet validated this mission through decades of execution across multiple crises at the level of a 4-point mission. Community Impact (1/2): ServiceNow has community programs and corporate philanthropy but no standout distinctive programs with specific dollar amounts or unique measurable impact were identified in search results. Employee Retention (2/2): ServiceNow has been named to the Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For, Forbes Most Trusted Companies in America, American Opportunity Index, and Fortune World's Best Workplaces lists [2]. McDermott's 'hungry and humble' culture and people-before-profits philosophy have created a distinctive work environment where employees cite mission alignment as a reason they stay. These multiple independent recognition sources provide strong evidence of above-average retention. Insider Buying (0/2): No evidence of meaningful net insider buying by CEO McDermott or other executives was identified in the last 12 months.

infrastructure.label: STRONG
infrastructure.analysis: Mechanically scored on available metrics — ServiceNow achieves near-perfect marks across all Infrastructure dimensions. Q1 2026 delivered $3.77 billion total revenue (up 22% YoY) and $3.67 billion subscription revenue (up 22% YoY), beating the high end of guidance across all metrics [1]. D/E of 20.7% is well below the 70% target with $5.2 billion cash against $2.4 billion debt. ROE of 16.1% exceeds the 15% target. Revenue growth YoY of 22.1% more than doubles the 10% target, and the 3-year revenue CAGR of 22.3% is equally strong. Forward P/E of 20.3 is essentially at the 20 target. Forward PEG of 0.99 is right at the 1.0 target, indicating the market is pricing in growth almost exactly proportional to its PE ratio [5]. The sole weakness is profit margin of 12.6% (below the 20% target), reflecting ServiceNow's continued investment in growth, stock-based compensation, and R&D spending. Free cash flow of $5.1 billion demonstrates strong underlying cash generation.

ai_resilience.label: LOW RISK
ai_resilience.analysis: AI Risk Protection (7/9): ServiceNow's core enterprise workflow platform is PROTECTED from AI disruption — the Now Platform is deeply embedded in enterprise IT infrastructure with massive switching costs (years of customized workflows, integrations, data, and organizational dependencies), regulatory requirements in ITSM and security operations, and a proprietary data moat across thousands of enterprise deployments [1][2]. Rather than being disrupted by AI, ServiceNow is actively deploying AI within its platform through Now Assist, becoming the 'AI control tower for business reinvention' as McDermott positions it. AI Governance features launched for EU AI Act compliance demonstrate proactive adaptation [2]. The primary risk is that generative AI could potentially commoditize basic IT ticket routing and workflow automation, but ServiceNow's embedded enterprise position and platform network effects make wholesale displacement highly unlikely. AI Opportunity (1/1): ServiceNow is generating meaningful AI revenue — Now Assist customers spending over $1 million in annual contract value grew 130% YoY in Q1 2026, indicating substantial and accelerating AI monetization [1]. The company is building AI-native features across every product line.

infinite_game.summary: ServiceNow under Bill McDermott is an exemplary infinite game player. McDermott's 'hungry and humble' philosophy, people-before-profits leadership style, and consistent articulation of making the world work better for everyone reflect deeply infinite thinking. The company has demonstrated willingness to invest heavily in long-term capabilities (AI, platform expansion, talent) while maintaining a culture repeatedly recognized as among the world's best workplaces. McDermott's track record across SAP (17 years) and ServiceNow demonstrates the kind of multi-decade consistency Sinek values most.

just_cause.analysis: Just Cause (2/3): 'Make the world work better for everyone' is a genuine Just Cause — inclusive (everyone, not just customers), service-oriented (improving how people work), idealistic (a world where work just works), and big enough to never be fully achieved [2]. McDermott consistently references this purpose in strategic decisions, from expanding into HR and customer service to building AI Governance features. The cause shapes behavior — ServiceNow's product expansion into every enterprise workflow traces back to this vision. However, at 22 years old and under McDermott's leadership for only 7 years, the cause has not yet been validated through multiple leadership transitions and crises at the level of a canonical Sinek example.

trusting_teams.analysis: Trusting Teams (2/2): ServiceNow's culture is independently validated by multiple prestigious recognition programs — Fortune 100 Best Companies to Work For, Fortune World's Best Workplaces, Forbes Most Trusted Companies in America, and American Opportunity Index [2]. McDermott's 'people before profits' philosophy and 'hungry and humble' cultural mantra create an environment where employees feel valued and empowered. The company's 29,187 employees operate in a culture consistently cited as a competitive advantage, with employees describing feeling trusted and inspired by the mission. This level of independent cultural validation across multiple sources is rare and represents strong evidence of high trust.

worthy_rivals.analysis: Worthy Rivals (1/1): ServiceNow under McDermott consistently frames competition as motivation for self-improvement rather than a war to win. McDermott's competitive language focuses on 'creating always' and building the best platform rather than destroying competitors. ServiceNow acknowledges strengths of rivals in adjacent markets (Salesforce in CRM, Microsoft in productivity) and uses competitive pressure to expand its own capabilities rather than attacking competitors' weaknesses [2].

existential_flexibility.analysis: Existential Flexibility (1/2): ServiceNow has significantly expanded from its ITSM origins into a comprehensive enterprise workflow platform spanning HR, customer service, security operations, and AI-powered agentic workflows [2]. Under McDermott, the company has evolved its positioning from 'IT tool' to 'platform for enterprise digital transformation.' However, the underlying business model (cloud-based subscription platform) has remained consistent — ServiceNow has not fundamentally reinvented itself or abandoned a profitable business in the way Sinek's canonical examples describe. The expansion is impressive but incremental rather than transformative.

courage_to_lead.analysis: Courage to Lead (2/2): McDermott has demonstrated a pattern of courageous long-term decisions at ServiceNow. He bet heavily on AI (Now Assist) before the AI market was proven for enterprise workflows, investing significantly in engineering and product development when ROI was uncertain [2]. He proactively launched AI Governance features for EU AI Act compliance before regulations were finalized, choosing principled leadership over waiting for regulatory clarity. The sustained investment in platform expansion — from ITSM into HR, customer service, security, and now agentic AI — represents capital allocation that consistently prioritizes long-term capability over short-term margin optimization, as reflected in the 12.6% profit margin despite $5.1 billion in free cash flow [1].

investment_thesis: ServiceNow is the dominant enterprise workflow automation platform, delivering Q1 2026 revenue of $3.77 billion (up 22% YoY) with subscription revenue of $3.67 billion (up 22% YoY), beating the high end of guidance across all metrics and raising full-year 2026 subscription guidance to $15.74-15.78 billion [1]. The company's financial profile is exceptional — D/E of 20.7%, $5.2 billion cash, $5.1 billion free cash flow, and a Forward PEG of 0.99 indicating the market is pricing growth proportionally to valuation. Now Assist AI customers spending >$1M ACV grew 130% YoY, validating ServiceNow's AI monetization strategy [1]. Remaining performance obligations of $27.7 billion (up 25% YoY) provide extraordinary revenue visibility. Under McDermott's leadership, ServiceNow has tripled revenue since 2019 while building a culture recognized among the world's best workplaces [2].
thesis_continued: The primary concern is ServiceNow's trailing P/E of 60.8 and GAAP profit margin of only 12.6%, reflecting significant stock-based compensation and continued heavy investment in growth [5]. While the Forward P/E of 20.3 and PEG of 0.99 appear reasonable, any deceleration in the 22% subscription growth rate could trigger multiple compression from the premium valuation. The company's dependence on large enterprise IT budgets makes it cyclically sensitive to macroeconomic conditions and enterprise spending freezes. Competition from Microsoft (which is aggressively embedding AI across its enterprise stack), Salesforce, and emerging AI-native workflow tools could pressure ServiceNow's market share over time. Additionally, the EU AI Act and evolving AI regulation create compliance costs and could constrain ServiceNow's AI product development velocity relative to less-regulated competitors.
key_catalysts: Now Assist AI monetization accelerating with >$1M ACV customers growing 130% YoY, positioning ServiceNow as the enterprise AI workflow standard and driving incremental subscription revenue growth above the 22% base rate

Remaining performance obligations of $27.7 billion (up 25% YoY) provide 18-24 months of revenue visibility and support management's raised full-year 2026 guidance of $15.74-15.78 billion in subscription revenue

Platform expansion into agentic AI workflows, AI Governance, and RaptorDB positions ServiceNow to capture a larger share of enterprise IT spending as organizations consolidate AI capabilities onto a single workflow platform
key_risks: Trailing P/E of 60.8 and GAAP profit margin of 12.6% create valuation vulnerability — any deceleration in the 22% subscription revenue growth rate could trigger significant multiple compression from premium levels

Microsoft's aggressive AI integration across Dynamics 365, Power Platform, and Copilot creates competitive pressure as enterprises evaluate whether to consolidate workflow automation within the Microsoft ecosystem rather than licensing ServiceNow separately

EU AI Act compliance costs and evolving global AI regulation could constrain ServiceNow's AI product development velocity and add operational complexity, particularly for AI Governance features that must adapt to rapidly evolving regulatory requirements
